problems.name: Warping
problems.description: Print corners or edges lift off the bed during printing, causing deformation
visual_symptoms: Corners of the print curling upward away from the bed
Print separating from bed surface at edges during print
Bottom layers visibly bowed or lifted at perimeter

probable_causes.cause: Bed temperature too low
probable_causes.test: Check bed temperature on display vs slicer setting

probable_causes.cause: Enclosure not used for high-temp materials
probable_causes.test: Check ambient temperature around printer during print

probable_causes.cause: First layer not adhered properly
probable_causes.test: Observe first layer — does it look flat and well-bonded?

probable_causes.cause: Large flat print surface with no brim
probable_causes.test: Check slicer for brim/raft usage on large footprint models

steps.instruction: Increase bed temperature by 5-10°C for PLA (60°C), ABS (100-110°C), PETG (80°C)
steps.verification: Display shows new target temperature

steps.instruction: Enable brim in slicer (5-10mm wide)
steps.verification: Slicer preview shows brim around model base

steps.instruction: Clean bed with isopropyl alcohol 90%+ before print
steps.verification: Bed surface is clean and grease-free

steps.instruction: Re-slice and reprint
steps.verification: Corners remain flat during first 5 layers

steps.instruction: Apply glue stick or hairspray to bed surface
steps.verification: Thin even layer visible on bed

steps.instruction: Add draft shield in slicer to reduce ambient air currents
steps.verification: Draft shield visible around model in slicer preview

steps.instruction: Use enclosure or cardboard tent to maintain ambient temperature
steps.verification: Ambient temperature stabilized at 30°C+

problems.name: Poor First Layer Adhesion
problems.description: First layer fails to stick to the bed surface, causing the print to detach early
visual_symptoms: First layer peels up or moves as nozzle travels over it
Gaps and holes visible in first layer when viewed from above
Extruded lines are rounded on top instead of pressed flat

probable_causes.cause: Z offset too high (nozzle too far from bed)
probable_causes.test: Observe first layer — does filament look squished or round?

probable_causes.cause: Bed surface dirty or contaminated
probable_causes.test: Inspect bed under light for grease or dust

probable_causes.cause: Bed temperature too low for material
probable_causes.test: Check nozzle and bed temp match recommended for filament

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too fast for first layer
probable_causes.test: Check first layer speed in slicer settings

steps.instruction: Clean bed with isopropyl alcohol 90%+ using a lint-free cloth
steps.verification: Bed looks clean, no residue visible

steps.instruction: Adjust Z offset down by 0.05-0.1mm increments (Live Adjust Z)
steps.verification: First layer lines look flat and slightly squished

steps.instruction: Reduce first layer speed to 20-25mm/s in slicer
steps.verification: Slicer shows updated first layer speed

steps.instruction: Verify bed temperature matches material recommendation
steps.verification: Display shows correct temperature

problems.name: Elephant's Foot
problems.description: Bottom layers of the print are wider than the rest, creating a flared base
visual_symptoms: First 1-3 layers visibly wider than the layers above
Outward bulge at the base of the print visible from the side
Model base does not match expected dimensions when measured

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle too close to bed (Z offset too low)
probable_causes.test: Check if first layer looks extremely flat and squished outward

probable_causes.cause: Bed temperature too high, softening bottom layers
probable_causes.test: Verify bed temperature is within recommended range

probable_causes.cause: First layer flow rate too high
probable_causes.test: Check first layer flow/extrusion multiplier in slicer

steps.instruction: Raise Z offset by 0.02-0.05mm increments
steps.verification: First layer lines look flat but not spreading sideways

steps.instruction: Reduce bed temperature by 5°C
steps.verification: Display shows new temperature and print base doesn't bulge

steps.instruction: Reduce first layer flow rate to 90-95% in slicer
steps.verification: Slicer shows updated first layer flow

problems.name: First Layer Too High
problems.description: Nozzle prints first layer too far from bed, leading to poor adhesion and gaps
visual_symptoms: First layer filament looks rounded (not squished) when viewed from side
Gaps between first layer lines visible from above
Print easily peels off bed with minimal force

probable_causes.cause: Z offset value set too high
probable_causes.test: Check Z offset value in printer menu — should be slightly negative

probable_causes.cause: Bed leveling mesh not applied correctly
probable_causes.test: Trigger bed leveling routine and observe mesh correction values

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle or bed surface changed without re-leveling
probable_causes.test: When was last bed leveling done?

steps.instruction: Run bed auto-leveling/tramming routine
steps.verification: Leveling completes without errors

steps.instruction: Decrease Z offset by 0.05mm increments while printing first layer
steps.verification: First layer lines appear flat and slightly squished

steps.instruction: Save Z offset to EEPROM / printer memory
steps.verification: Setting persists after power cycle

problems.name: First Layer Too Low
problems.description: Nozzle prints too close to bed, scraping surface and blocking extrusion
visual_symptoms: Nozzle scraping or dragging across bed surface (audible scratching)
No filament extruding during first layer or very little
Bed surface scratched or damaged after failed first layer

probable_causes.cause: Z offset set too low (too negative)
probable_causes.test: Listen for scraping sound during first layer; check Z offset value

probable_causes.cause: Manual bed leveling done incorrectly (too tight)
probable_causes.test: Paper test: paper should slide with slight resistance, not be locked

probable_causes.cause: Bed surface expanded from heat, raising bed
probable_causes.test: Level bed at print temperature, not cold

steps.instruction: Cancel print immediately to avoid bed damage
steps.verification: Print stopped

steps.instruction: Increase Z offset by 0.1mm increments
steps.verification: Nozzle clears bed surface without touching

steps.instruction: Run first layer calibration print at new Z offset
steps.verification: Nozzle doesn't scrape, filament extrudes normally

problems.name: Bed Not Level
problems.description: Bed is tilted or uneven, causing inconsistent first layer height across the print area
visual_symptoms: First layer looks good in one corner but too high or too low in another
Print adheres on one side but lifts on the opposite side
First layer thickness visibly inconsistent across different areas

probable_causes.cause: Manual tramming not performed recently
probable_causes.test: Paper test at all 4 corners — resistance should be equal

probable_causes.cause: Auto-leveling probe failed or needs calibration
probable_causes.test: Run manual leveling to verify mesh accuracy

probable_causes.cause: Bed warped or physically bent
probable_causes.test: Use a straight edge ruler across bed surface to check flatness

probable_causes.cause: Bed springs/springs uneven or worn
probable_causes.test: Check bed corner adjusters for equal tension

steps.instruction: Run auto bed leveling (ABL/UBL) routine
steps.verification: Mesh generated without errors

steps.instruction: Manually tram bed corners using paper test at each corner
steps.verification: Paper slides with equal resistance at all corners

steps.instruction: Reprint first layer test and observe uniformity
steps.verification: First layer consistent across full print area

problems.name: Nozzle Too Close to Bed
problems.description: Nozzle is extremely close to bed, causing scraping, blockages and bed damage
visual_symptoms: Visible scratches or gouges in PEI or glass bed surface
Filament not extruding or extruding in very thin amounts
Nozzle moves but leaves no or almost no trace of filament

probable_causes.cause: Z offset too negative after recent change
probable_causes.test: Check Z offset value — if more negative than -2mm on most printers, likely too low

probable_causes.cause: Bed physically moved closer (spring compressed too much)
probable_causes.test: Home Z and manually check clearance between nozzle and bed

probable_causes.cause: Different bed surface thickness installed without re-calibrating
probable_causes.test: Was bed surface recently changed (glass → PEI or vice versa)?

steps.instruction: Stop print immediately
steps.verification: Print halted

steps.instruction: Navigate to Z offset in printer menu and increase value by 0.1-0.2mm
steps.verification: Z offset shows higher (less negative) value

steps.instruction: Home Z axis and verify nozzle has clearance from bed
steps.verification: A sheet of paper fits between nozzle and bed with slight resistance

steps.instruction: Inspect bed for damage; if PEI is scratched, smooth with fine sandpaper
steps.verification: Bed surface is smooth

problems.name: Nozzle Too Far from Bed
problems.description: Nozzle prints too high above bed surface, filament doesn't bond to bed
visual_symptoms: Filament strands look round and not pressed into bed
First layer lines are separate and not bonded to each other
Completed first layer can be pulled off easily in one piece

probable_causes.cause: Z offset set too high (too positive or not negative enough)
probable_causes.test: Observe first layer — filament should be slightly squished

probable_causes.cause: Bed probe z-offset (probe trigger height) miscalibrated
probable_causes.test: Re-run probe calibration routine

probable_causes.cause: Bed physically lower than expected (spring stretched or loose)
probable_causes.test: Check bed corner screws and spring tension

steps.instruction: Decrease Z offset by 0.05mm increments during live print
steps.verification: First layer filament looks flat and bonded to bed

steps.instruction: Save adjusted Z offset
steps.verification: Setting persists

problems.name: Stringing / Oozing
problems.description: Thin strings of filament left between separate parts of the print during travel moves
visual_symptoms: Thin hair-like strings stretched between separate parts of the print
Cobweb-like filament deposits between towers or protrusions
Fine hairs visible on the print surface near travel paths

probable_causes.cause: Retraction distance too low
probable_causes.test: Check retraction distance in slicer — typical: 1-2mm direct drive, 4-6mm bowden

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle temperature too high
probable_causes.test: Check nozzle temperature — high temps increase oozing

probable_causes.cause: Travel speed too slow
probable_causes.test: Check travel speed in slicer — should be 150-200mm/s

probable_causes.cause: Wet filament absorbing moisture
probable_causes.test: Listen for popping or crackling sounds during extrusion

steps.instruction: Lower nozzle temperature by 5-10°C
steps.verification: Temperature display shows new value

steps.instruction: Increase retraction distance: 1-2mm (direct drive) or 4-6mm (Bowden)
steps.verification: Slicer shows updated retraction distance

steps.instruction: Increase retraction speed to 40-60mm/s
steps.verification: Slicer shows updated retraction speed

steps.instruction: Increase travel speed to 150-200mm/s
steps.verification: Slicer shows updated travel speed

steps.instruction: Run stringing test print (temperature tower or travel test)
steps.verification: Fewer or no strings visible between towers

problems.name: Under-Extrusion
problems.description: Printer deposits less filament than expected, resulting in gaps and weak prints
visual_symptoms: Gaps between extrusion lines visible in walls and infill
Layers appear thin or incomplete with missing sections
Print feels light and fragile; walls show pinholes

probable_causes.cause: Partial clog or dirty nozzle
probable_causes.test: Manually extrude 100mm filament and measure actual output

probable_causes.cause: Extruder not calibrated (E-steps incorrect)
probable_causes.test: Mark filament 100mm above extruder and command 100mm extrusion; measure remainder

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too high for temperature
probable_causes.test: Reduce print speed by 25% and observe if gaps reduce

probable_causes.cause: Filament diameter mismatch in slicer
probable_causes.test: Measure actual filament diameter with calipers at multiple points

steps.instruction: Increase flow rate / extrusion multiplier by 5% in slicer
steps.verification: Walls look more solid with fewer gaps

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed by 20%
steps.verification: Slicer shows reduced speed; gaps diminish

steps.instruction: Check nozzle temperature — increase by 5°C if at minimum
steps.verification: Temperature at or above minimum recommended

steps.instruction: Calibrate E-steps: command 100mm extrusion, measure actual output
steps.verification: Actual extrusion within 1mm of commanded 100mm

steps.instruction: Measure filament diameter at 5 points with calipers, enter average in slicer
steps.verification: Slicer filament diameter matches measured value

steps.instruction: Perform cold pull to check for partial nozzle clog
steps.verification: Cold pull filament tip shows nozzle bore shape without debris

problems.name: Over-Extrusion
problems.description: Printer deposits more filament than needed, causing bumpy surfaces and dimensional inaccuracy
visual_symptoms: Lumpy or bumpy top and side surfaces
Seam area has visible blobs or excess material
Printed parts are larger than designed dimensions

probable_causes.cause: Flow rate / extrusion multiplier set too high
probable_causes.test: Check flow rate in slicer — should be 95-105% for most materials

probable_causes.cause: E-steps calibrated too high
probable_causes.test: Extrude 100mm and measure actual output — should be 100mm

probable_causes.cause: Filament diameter smaller than entered in slicer
probable_causes.test: Measure filament diameter with calipers

steps.instruction: Reduce flow rate / extrusion multiplier by 5% in slicer
steps.verification: Surfaces look smoother, print dimensions more accurate

steps.instruction: Measure filament diameter and update slicer setting
steps.verification: Slicer diameter matches measured value

problems.name: Clogged Nozzle
problems.description: Nozzle is fully blocked, preventing any filament from extruding
visual_symptoms: No filament coming out of nozzle during extrusion commands
Extruder motor grinding or clicking without any extrusion
Print stopped mid-print with no material being deposited

probable_causes.cause: Carbonized or burnt filament inside nozzle
probable_causes.test: Heat nozzle and try manual extrusion — any resistance or no output?

probable_causes.cause: Foreign material (dust, debris) in filament causing blockage
probable_causes.test: Inspect filament for debris or use filament filter

probable_causes.cause: Filament ground down in extruder before reaching nozzle
probable_causes.test: Check extruder gear for filament dust buildup

probable_causes.cause: Printing too cold causing material to solidify in nozzle
probable_causes.test: Verify print temperature matches filament specification

steps.instruction: Heat nozzle 10-20°C above normal print temp (e.g. 230°C for PLA)
steps.verification: Temperature reached

steps.instruction: Manually push filament through nozzle with light pressure
steps.verification: Filament flows out; no resistance

steps.instruction: Perform cold pull: heat to 200°C, push filament, cool to 90°C, pull firmly
steps.verification: Pulled filament tip has nozzle shape with debris embedded

steps.instruction: Remove nozzle (heat to 250°C first), soak in acetone for 1-2 hours
steps.verification: Nozzle soaked and debris dissolved

steps.instruction: Use 0.3mm needle to clear nozzle bore while hot
steps.verification: Needle passes through nozzle bore freely

steps.instruction: Reinstall nozzle while hot to prevent cross-threading; torque to 1-1.5Nm
steps.verification: Nozzle seated without leaks

steps.instruction: Test extrusion at normal temperature
steps.verification: Filament flows smoothly

problems.name: Partial Clog
problems.description: Nozzle is partially blocked, causing reduced and inconsistent extrusion
visual_symptoms: Extrusion is thinner than expected with some gaps but not fully stopped
Extruder motor occasionally clicks but not constantly
Print quality degrades over time as clog worsens

probable_causes.cause: Partial carbonization or debris in nozzle
probable_causes.test: Extrude 100mm manually and check if flow is consistent and full

probable_causes.cause: Printing at too low a temperature for the material
probable_causes.test: Try increasing temperature 5-10°C and observe if extrusion improves

probable_causes.cause: Mixed filament remnants from previous material
probable_causes.test: Did you recently change filament type?

steps.instruction: Heat nozzle 15°C above normal print temp
steps.verification: Temperature reached

steps.instruction: Manually extrude 50-100mm of filament at high temp to flush debris
steps.verification: Flow improves and becomes consistent

steps.instruction: Perform 2-3 cold pulls at 90-100°C (for PLA)
steps.verification: Pulled tip is clean without dark spots

problems.name: Filament Grinding
problems.description: Extruder gear chews into filament instead of feeding it, creating dust and stopping extrusion
visual_symptoms: Visible chewed notch or groove worn into filament near extruder
Plastic dust or shavings accumulating near extruder gear
Clicking sound from extruder motor as gear slips on damaged filament

probable_causes.cause: Extruder tension too high, biting into filament
probable_causes.test: Check extruder arm tension spring — can it be loosened slightly?

probable_causes.cause: Partial clog causing back-pressure on extruder
probable_causes.test: Remove Bowden or hotend and try extruding — does resistance disappear?

probable_causes.cause: Print speed or flow rate too high for temperature
probable_causes.test: Reduce print speed by 30% and observe if grinding stops

probable_causes.cause: Extruder motor current too low
probable_causes.test: Check TMC driver current settings for extruder motor

steps.instruction: Cancel print, heat nozzle, manually pull out remaining filament
steps.verification: Filament removed without resistance

steps.instruction: Inspect filament for chewed section; cut above damaged area
steps.verification: Fresh clean filament end visible

steps.instruction: Reload filament and reduce print speed by 20-30%
steps.verification: Filament feeds smoothly without clicking

steps.instruction: Check for and resolve any nozzle partial clog
steps.verification: Filament extrudes freely

problems.name: Inconsistent Extrusion
problems.description: Extrusion varies in width causing wavy, uneven lines and poor surface quality
visual_symptoms: Extruded lines vary in width — alternating thick and thin sections
Wavy or uneven appearance on walls and surfaces
Moire or wood-grain pattern visible on print walls

probable_causes.cause: Filament diameter inconsistency
probable_causes.test: Measure filament diameter every 10cm over 50cm with calipers

probable_causes.cause: Partial clog causing flow variation
probable_causes.test: Extrude slowly and observe uniformity of flow

probable_causes.cause: Extruder tension arm loose or inconsistent grip
probable_causes.test: Check extruder idler tension — should grip firmly without crushing filament

probable_causes.cause: PTFE tube damage or gap at nozzle coupling
probable_causes.test: Inspect Bowden/PTFE tube for kinks, damage, or gaps at hotend coupling

steps.instruction: Measure filament at 10 points and check for diameter variation > 0.05mm
steps.verification: Diameter measurements consistent within ±0.05mm

steps.instruction: Inspect and reseat PTFE tube — ensure no gap at nozzle end
steps.verification: Tube seats firmly against nozzle; no gap

steps.instruction: Check extruder idler tension and adjust to manufacturer spec
steps.verification: Filament gripped firmly; no slipping during manual extrusion

problems.name: Blobs and Zits
problems.description: Small bumps or blobs appear on print surface, especially at layer start/end points
visual_symptoms: Small round bumps on outer wall surface at seam locations
Tiny pimple-like protrusions at random points on walls
Visible seam with excess material at layer start/stop

probable_causes.cause: Incorrect retraction settings causing ooze at seam
probable_causes.test: Check retraction distance and speed — and seam position setting

probable_causes.cause: Coasting not enabled or set incorrectly
probable_causes.test: Check if coasting is enabled in slicer advanced settings

probable_causes.cause: Pressure advance / linear advance not calibrated
probable_causes.test: Print PA/LA calibration pattern and observe corners

probable_causes.cause: Start/stop speed too slow causing material accumulation
probable_causes.test: Check seam position set to random or rear to hide seam

steps.instruction: Enable and calibrate Pressure Advance (Klipper) or Linear Advance (Marlin)
steps.verification: PA/LA calibration print shows square corners without bulging

steps.instruction: Set seam position to 'rear' or 'random' in slicer
steps.verification: Seam moved to less visible location

steps.instruction: Enable coasting in slicer (0.2-0.5mm3)
steps.verification: Less excess material at seam

problems.name: Wet / Moisture-Absorbed Filament
problems.description: Filament has absorbed moisture from air, causing poor print quality and surface defects
visual_symptoms: Popping, crackling, or hissing sounds during extrusion
Steam or tiny bubbles visible at nozzle tip during extrusion
Rough, fuzzy, or bubbly surface texture on printed parts
Brittle filament that snaps easily when bent

probable_causes.cause: Filament stored without desiccant in humid environment
probable_causes.test: Check filament storage — was it in a sealed bag or dry box?

probable_causes.cause: Hygroscopic material (Nylon, PETG, TPU) absorbing ambient humidity
probable_causes.test: Listen for crackling during extrusion; check humidity of print environment

probable_causes.cause: Filament left on printer for extended period
probable_causes.test: How long was the spool sitting on the printer without a cover?

steps.instruction: Dry filament in food dehydrator or oven: PLA 45°C/4h, PETG 65°C/4h, Nylon 70°C/8h
steps.verification: Crackling sounds stop during extrusion after drying

steps.instruction: Store filament in sealed container with silica gel desiccant
steps.verification: Container sealed with desiccant; humidity indicator shows <20%

problems.name: Filament Not Feeding
problems.description: Filament fails to advance through the extruder, halting extrusion completely
visual_symptoms: Extruder motor turning but filament not moving
No filament visible being deposited despite print running
Extruder making grinding or clicking sound without filament advancement

probable_causes.cause: Filament run-out or empty spool
probable_causes.test: Check spool — is there filament remaining?

probable_causes.cause: Filament tangle on spool
probable_causes.test: Unravel spool manually — does a loop free itself?

probable_causes.cause: Filament grinding (stripped) at extruder
probable_causes.test: Inspect filament near extruder gear for chewed section

probable_causes.cause: Bowden tube disconnected or blocked
probable_causes.test: Check Bowden tube connection at both ends

probable_causes.cause: Extruder motor driver failure
probable_causes.test: Listen to extruder motor — is it receiving power and making sound?

steps.instruction: Check spool and untangle any filament loops
steps.verification: Filament feeds freely from spool

steps.instruction: Inspect filament near extruder; cut above any chewed section
steps.verification: Fresh filament end visible

steps.instruction: Reload filament: heat nozzle, push filament until it extrudes from tip
steps.verification: Filament exits nozzle smoothly

steps.instruction: Check Bowden tube connections if applicable
steps.verification: Tube firmly seated at both ends

problems.name: Layer Shifting
problems.description: Print layers shift horizontally mid-print, causing offset bands in the model
visual_symptoms: Visible horizontal offset in the print — upper layers displaced from lower ones
Print appears stepped or staircase-like from the side
Layer shift may worsen as print continues higher

probable_causes.cause: Loose or skipping X/Y belts
probable_causes.test: Pluck belts like guitar strings — should sound similar; check tension

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too high for acceleration settings
probable_causes.test: Reduce print speed by 30% and observe if shifting stops

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle collision with model (blob or warped section)
probable_causes.test: Inspect failed print for blobs or collision damage

probable_causes.cause: Stepper motor current too low
probable_causes.test: Check TMC driver current for X/Y motors — compare to manufacturer spec

steps.instruction: Check belt tension on X and Y axes — tighten if slack
steps.verification: Belts taut and produce similar pitch when plucked

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed by 25% and acceleration by 20%
steps.verification: Slicer shows reduced values

steps.instruction: Check for any blobs or rough spots on model that could cause nozzle collision
steps.verification: Print surface smooth; enable Z-hop in slicer

problems.name: Ghosting / Ringing
problems.description: Rippling or wave patterns appear on print walls after sharp corners or features
visual_symptoms: Wave or ripple pattern on walls after corners or holes
Ghost image of a feature appears on nearby walls
Oscillating lines most visible on outer walls near sharp transitions

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too high causing vibration/resonance
probable_causes.test: Reduce speed by 30% — does ringing reduce?

probable_causes.cause: Input shaping / resonance compensation not calibrated
probable_causes.test: Run ringing test print at current settings and compare to with input shaping

probable_causes.cause: Loose belts allowing mechanical resonance
probable_causes.test: Check belt tension — pluck and compare pitch

probable_causes.cause: Printer on unstable surface absorbing vibration
probable_causes.test: Place printer on solid level surface and retry

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed to 50-60mm/s
steps.verification: Ringing reduced at lower speed

steps.instruction: Reduce acceleration to 500-1000mm/s²
steps.verification: Fewer waves on wall surfaces

steps.instruction: Calibrate Input Shaping (Klipper) using ADXL345 accelerometer
steps.verification: Input shaping calibration print shows reduced ringing

steps.instruction: Tighten all belts to correct tension spec
steps.verification: Belt pluck produces correct frequency

problems.name: Z Banding / Zebra Lines
problems.description: Repeating horizontal bands or lines appear at regular intervals on print walls
visual_symptoms: Horizontal bands repeating at consistent intervals up the print
Alternating light and dark stripes on outer walls
Pattern repeats every 1-2mm corresponding to lead screw pitch

probable_causes.cause: Z lead screw wobble or bent lead screw
probable_causes.test: Watch Z screw rotate during print — does it wobble?

probable_causes.cause: Z lead screw nut loose or eccentric
probable_causes.test: Check Z nut mount for play or looseness

probable_causes.cause: Lead screw coupler loose or misaligned
probable_causes.test: Check coupler between stepper and lead screw — any wobble?

probable_causes.cause: Inconsistent Z motor current causing micro-step irregularity
probable_causes.test: Check Z motor current setting

steps.instruction: Check and tighten Z axis lead screw coupler set screws
steps.verification: No play between motor shaft and coupler

steps.instruction: Lubricate lead screws with PTFE-based grease or super lube
steps.verification: Lead screw moves smoothly without binding

steps.instruction: Install anti-backlash Z nut if not present
steps.verification: Z movement smooth with no backlash

steps.instruction: Verify lead screw is straight — roll on flat surface
steps.verification: Lead screw doesn't wobble when rolled

problems.name: Poor Bridging
problems.description: Horizontal spans between two supports sag or fail, creating drooping or messy bridges
visual_symptoms: Sagging or drooping filament strands on horizontal spans
Stringy, messy underside on bridge sections
Bridge fails completely — filament drops into open air gap

probable_causes.cause: Bridge speed too slow (filament sags before cooling)
probable_causes.test: Increase bridging speed and observe if strands stay taut

probable_causes.cause: Cooling not sufficient for bridging
probable_causes.test: Check part cooling fan is running at 100% during bridges

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle temperature too high for bridging
probable_causes.test: Lower temperature 5-10°C and test bridge performance

probable_causes.cause: Bridge too long without intermediate supports
probable_causes.test: Measure bridge span — over 50mm typically needs supports

steps.instruction: Set bridging fan speed to 100% in slicer
steps.verification: Fan at full speed during bridge layers

steps.instruction: Set bridge speed to 30-50mm/s in slicer
steps.verification: Bridges print faster with less sag

steps.instruction: Lower temperature by 5°C for bridge layers
steps.verification: Material cools faster and stays taut

problems.name: Gaps in Top Layers
problems.description: Top surface of print has holes, gaps or incomplete fill, showing infill below
visual_symptoms: Holes or gaps visible in top surface, especially near center
Infill pattern showing through top layer
Top surface looks mesh-like or porous instead of solid

probable_causes.cause: Not enough top solid layers
probable_causes.test: Check top layers count in slicer — should be 4-5 minimum

probable_causes.cause: Under-extrusion causing incomplete top fill
probable_causes.test: Check if walls also show under-extrusion symptoms

probable_causes.cause: Top surface line width too wide or too narrow
probable_causes.test: Check top/bottom line width in slicer advanced settings

probable_causes.cause: Infill percentage too low causing top layers to bridge over gaps
probable_causes.test: Check infill — below 20% can cause top surface issues

steps.instruction: Increase top layers count to 5-6 in slicer
steps.verification: Slicer preview shows more solid layers on top

steps.instruction: Increase infill to 20-25% if lower
steps.verification: Better support for top layers

steps.instruction: Increase flow rate by 3-5%
steps.verification: Top layers look more complete

problems.name: Rough / Uneven Top Surface
problems.description: Top surface of print looks rough, bumpy or grainy instead of smooth
visual_symptoms: Visible line patterns or texture on top surface
Bumpy or granular appearance on top face
Top surface lines not fusing smoothly together

probable_causes.cause: Top surface print speed too high
probable_causes.test: Check top surface speed in slicer — reduce to 30-40mm/s

probable_causes.cause: Ironing not enabled for smooth top finish
probable_causes.test: Check if ironing feature is available and enabled in slicer

probable_causes.cause: Over-extrusion causing bumps
probable_causes.test: Check flow rate and observe wall surface for over-extrusion signs

probable_causes.cause: Insufficient cooling on top layers
probable_causes.test: Check cooling fan speed during top layer printing

steps.instruction: Reduce top surface print speed to 30-40mm/s
steps.verification: Top surface looks smoother at lower speed

steps.instruction: Enable ironing in slicer at 0.1mm ironing line spacing
steps.verification: Slicer shows ironing pass on top surfaces

steps.instruction: Ensure cooling fan at 100% during top layers
steps.verification: Smoother solidification of top surface

problems.name: Visible Layer Lines
problems.description: Layer lines clearly visible on print surface, reducing visual quality
visual_symptoms: Horizontal ridge lines visible on vertical print surfaces
Staircase effect on curved or angled surfaces
Overall print surface looks layered rather than smooth

probable_causes.cause: Layer height too large
probable_causes.test: Check layer height in slicer — 0.2mm is standard, 0.1mm reduces visibility

probable_causes.cause: Adaptive layer height not enabled on curved features
probable_causes.test: Check if variable layer height is available in slicer

probable_causes.cause: Part not oriented to minimize layer lines on critical surfaces
probable_causes.test: Consider rotating model to place critical surface parallel to bed

steps.instruction: Reduce layer height to 0.1-0.12mm for fine detail prints
steps.verification: Layer lines less visible in slicer preview and on print

steps.instruction: Reorient model so critical surfaces print vertically (not on top/bottom)
steps.verification: Critical surface smooth in slicer preview

steps.instruction: Sand print with progressive grits (120 → 220 → 400) and prime
steps.verification: Layer lines no longer visible after sanding

problems.name: Overhangs Drooping
problems.description: Print material on overhang sections sags or droops downward due to insufficient support
visual_symptoms: Drooping or sagging material on sections extending beyond 45-50° overhang
Rough, stringy underside on overhanging surfaces
Curling upward on edges of steep overhangs

probable_causes.cause: Overhang angle exceeds printer capability without support
probable_causes.test: Check overhang angle — over 45-50° typically needs support or good cooling

probable_causes.cause: Cooling insufficient for overhang layers
probable_causes.test: Check if part cooling fan is at 100% during overhang layers

probable_causes.cause: Temperature too high for good overhang performance
probable_causes.test: Lower temperature 5°C and test on overhang test print

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too fast on overhang sections
probable_causes.test: Check if overhang speed reduction is enabled in slicer

steps.instruction: Set cooling fan to 100% for all layers
steps.verification: Fan running at full speed during overhang layers

steps.instruction: Enable support structures in slicer for overhangs > 45°
steps.verification: Supports visible in slicer preview under steep overhangs

steps.instruction: Reduce overhang speed to 15-20mm/s in slicer
steps.verification: Slicer shows reduced speed for overhang perimeters

problems.name: Poor Infill Quality
problems.description: Infill is incomplete, disconnected or improperly bonded to walls
visual_symptoms: Infill lines not connecting to walls — visible gap between wall and infill
Sparse or incomplete infill despite adequate percentage setting
Infill lines crossing over each other causing over-extrusion blobs in infill

probable_causes.cause: Infill overlap too low (not bonding to walls)
probable_causes.test: Check infill overlap percentage in slicer — try 20-30%

probable_causes.cause: Infill print speed too high causing poor bonding
probable_causes.test: Reduce infill speed and observe if gaps decrease

probable_causes.cause: Under-extrusion during infill
probable_causes.test: Increase flow rate slightly and check infill density

probable_causes.cause: Wrong infill pattern for structural requirements
probable_causes.test: Try gyroid or honeycomb instead of lines for better strength

steps.instruction: Increase infill overlap to 25-30% in slicer
steps.verification: Infill visibly connects better to walls

steps.instruction: Reduce infill speed to 50-60mm/s
steps.verification: Fewer gaps in infill pattern

steps.instruction: Increase infill flow rate by 5% if gaps persist
steps.verification: More complete infill coverage

problems.name: Pillowing
problems.description: Top surface shows bumps or domes between infill lines due to insufficient top layers or cooling
visual_symptoms: Pillow-like bumps visible on top surface between infill support lines
Top surface looks puffy or wavy from above
Infill pattern faintly visible through uneven top surface

probable_causes.cause: Too few top layers
probable_causes.test: Count top solid layers in slicer — minimum 4-5 recommended

probable_causes.cause: Insufficient cooling causing top layers to soften
probable_causes.test: Check part cooling fan speed during top layers

probable_causes.cause: Infill density too low providing insufficient support for top surface
probable_causes.test: Check infill — below 15% can cause pillowing

probable_causes.cause: Top layer print temperature too high
probable_causes.test: Lower print temperature 5°C for top layers

steps.instruction: Increase top solid layers to 5-6 in slicer
steps.verification: Top surface fully solid with no bumps

steps.instruction: Increase infill density to 20-25%
steps.verification: Better structural support for top layers

steps.instruction: Set cooling fan to 100% for top layers
steps.verification: Top surface solidifies faster and lies flat

problems.name: Gaps Between Infill and Walls
problems.description: Visible gaps appear between the outer walls and the infill structure
visual_symptoms: Visible channels or gaps between perimeter walls and infill lines
Looking at top view or cross-section shows separation between walls and fill
Walls feel hollow or loose compared to expected solid feel

probable_causes.cause: Infill overlap with perimeter too low
probable_causes.test: Check infill overlap setting — increase to 25-35%

probable_causes.cause: Infill print speed much higher than perimeter speed causing width mismatch
probable_causes.test: Check speed delta between wall speed and infill speed

probable_causes.cause: Under-extrusion during infill lines
probable_causes.test: Increase flow rate and check if gaps close

steps.instruction: Increase infill-wall overlap to 30% in slicer settings
steps.verification: Cross-section preview shows infill connecting to walls

steps.instruction: Reduce infill speed delta — keep infill speed within 2x wall speed
steps.verification: More consistent line widths between wall and infill

steps.instruction: Increase flow rate by 3-5% if under-extrusion suspected
steps.verification: Gaps close on next print

problems.name: Scarring on Top Surface
problems.description: Nozzle leaves visible drag marks or scars on top surfaces during travel moves
visual_symptoms: Drag marks or grooves on completed top surface from nozzle travel
Lines scratched into otherwise smooth top surface
Blobs dragged across top layer leaving streaks

probable_causes.cause: Z-hop not enabled — nozzle drags on surface during travel
probable_causes.test: Check if Z-hop is enabled in slicer retraction settings

probable_causes.cause: Combing/avoid crossing perimeters not enabled
probable_causes.test: Enable combing in slicer to keep travel moves within infill area

probable_causes.cause: Over-extrusion causing excess material that nozzle drags over
probable_causes.test: Check for blobs or excess material on top surface

steps.instruction: Enable Z-hop on retraction (0.2-0.4mm) in slicer
steps.verification: Nozzle lifts during travel moves; no drag marks

steps.instruction: Enable combing / avoid crossing perimeters in slicer
steps.verification: Travel moves stay inside model boundaries

steps.instruction: Check and reduce flow rate if over-extrusion blobs present
steps.verification: Less material for nozzle to drag across

problems.name: Thermal Runaway
problems.description: Nozzle or bed temperature drops unexpectedly and printer triggers safety shutdown
visual_symptoms: Printer suddenly stops and displays 'Thermal Runaway' error message
Temperature reading drops suddenly or reads incorrectly on display
Print stops mid-way with no movement and error on screen

probable_causes.cause: Hotend thermistor loose or disconnected
probable_causes.test: Check thermistor wires and connector — any intermittent connections?

probable_causes.cause: Heater cartridge failing or insufficient wattage
probable_causes.test: Verify heater cartridge wattage matches printer spec; check for damage

probable_causes.cause: Fan blowing too much air on heater block
probable_causes.test: Check if part cooling fan or enclosure fan directs air onto heater block

probable_causes.cause: PID values miscalibrated — temperature oscillating too widely
probable_causes.test: Run PID autotune and observe temperature stability

steps.instruction: STOP: Do not bypass or disable thermal runaway protection — it is a fire safety feature
steps.verification: Thermal runaway protection remains enabled

steps.instruction: Inspect thermistor connection at hotend and control board — reseat connectors
steps.verification: Connections firm and secure; temperature reads stable

steps.instruction: Run PID autotune: M303 E0 S200 C8 (Marlin) or PID_CALIBRATE (Klipper)
steps.verification: PID values saved; temperature holds stable within ±1°C

steps.instruction: Verify heater block is insulated with thermal sock or kapton tape
steps.verification: Heater block insulated from air currents

steps.instruction: Replace thermistor if temperature readings are erratic
steps.verification: Temperature stable and accurate

problems.name: Heat Creep
problems.description: Heat migrates up the hotend into the cold zone, softening filament prematurely and causing jams
visual_symptoms: Print starts fine then gradually stops extruding after 20-60 minutes
Filament cannot be retracted or pushed — feels stuck in cold zone
Extruder clicking noise that increases over print duration

probable_causes.cause: Hotend cooling fan failing or spinning too slowly
probable_causes.test: Check if hotend fan runs at full speed during print; listen for grinding bearing

probable_causes.cause: PTFE liner degraded or improperly seated
probable_causes.test: Remove and inspect PTFE tube — any melted, discolored or warped sections?

probable_causes.cause: Printing too fast/hot for cooling capacity of hotend
probable_causes.test: Reduce temperature by 5-10°C and speed by 20%; observe if jam recurs

probable_causes.cause: Heatsink blocked with dust or filament debris
probable_causes.test: Inspect heatsink fins for debris or blockage

steps.instruction: Verify hotend cooling fan is spinning at full speed during print
steps.verification: Fan audibly at full speed; heatsink cool to touch below cooling fins

steps.instruction: Clean heatsink fins with compressed air or soft brush
steps.verification: No debris blocking airflow

steps.instruction: Replace hotend cooling fan if noisy or slow
steps.verification: New fan runs silently at correct speed

steps.instruction: Replace PTFE liner inside hotend if discolored or melted
steps.verification: New PTFE tube seated properly against nozzle

steps.instruction: Reduce print temperature by 5°C and speed by 15%
steps.verification: Print completes multi-hour jobs without heat creep recurrence

problems.name: Warping from Thermal Differential
problems.description: Print warps due to temperature difference between layers causing differential shrinkage
visual_symptoms: Large print corners lifting significantly — often 5-15mm off bed
Cracking sounds during printing as material contracts
Print detaches from bed entirely due to thermal stress

probable_causes.cause: No enclosure for high-temperature materials (ABS, ASA, PC)
probable_causes.test: Measure ambient temperature near print — should be 40-50°C for ABS

probable_causes.cause: Cooling fan too aggressive for warping-prone materials
probable_causes.test: Disable cooling fan for ABS/ASA and observe warping reduction

probable_causes.cause: Bed temperature too low
probable_causes.test: Increase bed temp: ABS needs 100-110°C, ASA 100°C+

probable_causes.cause: Material intrinsically high-shrinkage without chamber heating
probable_causes.test: Check material datasheet for shrinkage percentage

steps.instruction: Use a printer enclosure and preheat to 40-50°C before printing ABS/ASA
steps.verification: Ambient temperature inside enclosure reaches target

steps.instruction: Disable or reduce part cooling fan for ABS/ASA (0-20% max)
steps.verification: Warping significantly reduced

steps.instruction: Increase bed temperature to 100-110°C for ABS, 100°C for ASA
steps.verification: Display shows correct bed temperature

steps.instruction: Apply brim (8-10mm wide) to increase bed contact area
steps.verification: Print corners stay down throughout print

problems.name: Delamination / Layer Separation
problems.description: Print layers separate or split apart, creating visible cracks between layers
visual_symptoms: Visible cracks or splits running horizontally between print layers
Layers that can be peeled apart manually with little force
Print looks laminated with gaps visible between sections

probable_causes.cause: Print temperature too low for inter-layer adhesion
probable_causes.test: Increase temperature by 5-10°C and check if delamination stops

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too high for adequate layer bonding time
probable_causes.test: Reduce print speed by 30% and observe if layers bond better

probable_causes.cause: Layer height too large relative to nozzle diameter
probable_causes.test: Layer height should not exceed 75% of nozzle diameter

probable_causes.cause: Rapid cooling preventing interlayer fusion
probable_causes.test: Reduce cooling fan speed especially for first 10 layers

steps.instruction: Increase nozzle temperature by 5-10°C
steps.verification: Layers bond visibly better; no cracks between layers

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed to 40-50mm/s
steps.verification: More time for layers to fuse properly

steps.instruction: Reduce layer height if > 75% of nozzle diameter
steps.verification: Better layer bonding at reduced height

steps.instruction: Reduce cooling fan speed to 50-70% for first 10 layers
steps.verification: No delamination in lower sections of print

problems.name: Nozzle Temperature Fluctuation
problems.description: Nozzle temperature oscillates more than ±2°C causing inconsistent extrusion quality
visual_symptoms: Temperature graph showing wide oscillations (>±3°C) rather than flat line
Print surface quality varying in horizontal bands corresponding to temperature cycles
Clicking or inconsistent extrusion correlated with temperature dips

probable_causes.cause: PID values not tuned for current hotend
probable_causes.test: Run M303 PID autotune and compare current vs tuned values

probable_causes.cause: Heater cartridge loose in heater block
probable_causes.test: Check if heater cartridge is fully seated and its set screw tight

probable_causes.cause: Thermistor partially disconnected causing false readings
probable_causes.test: Wiggle thermistor wires and observe if temperature reading jumps

probable_causes.cause: Partial heater block damage or wrong voltage heater
probable_causes.test: Check heater cartridge voltage rating matches PSU output

steps.instruction: Run PID autotune: M303 E0 S200 C8 (Marlin) or PID_CALIBRATE TARGET=200 (Klipper)
steps.verification: New PID values; temperature holds within ±1°C

steps.instruction: Save PID values to EEPROM (M500 in Marlin) or update printer.cfg
steps.verification: Values persist after restart

steps.instruction: Tighten heater cartridge grub screw if loose
steps.verification: Heater cartridge fully seated and secure

problems.name: Bed Temperature Fluctuation
problems.description: Heated bed temperature varies significantly during print, affecting first layer adhesion
visual_symptoms: Bed temperature oscillating more than ±3°C on display during print
First layer inconsistency observed across different print attempts
Print adhesion failures correlating with temperature dip periods

probable_causes.cause: Bed PID not tuned (bang-bang mode causing oscillation)
probable_causes.test: Check if bed uses PID or bang-bang control — enable PID mode

probable_causes.cause: Bed power supply insufficient for large heated bed
probable_causes.test: Check PSU current rating vs. bed heater wattage

probable_causes.cause: Bed thermistor placement not representative of print surface temperature
probable_causes.test: Compare IR thermometer reading on bed surface vs display

probable_causes.cause: Voltage fluctuations from PSU affecting bed heater output
probable_causes.test: Measure PSU output voltage under bed heating load

steps.instruction: Enable and run PID tuning for heated bed (M303 E-1 S60 C8 in Marlin)
steps.verification: Bed temperature stable within ±1°C at target

steps.instruction: Save bed PID values to EEPROM
steps.verification: Values persist after reboot

steps.instruction: Verify PSU wattage is sufficient: bed wattage + hotend + motors + fans with 20% headroom
steps.verification: PSU runs cool and stable during full print

problems.name: Loose Belts
problems.description: X or Y axis drive belts are slack, causing layer shifting, ghosting and poor dimensional accuracy
visual_symptoms: Ringing/ghosting artifact on walls after sharp corners
Layer shifting in X or Y direction inconsistently
Dimensional inaccuracy — circles print as ovals

probable_causes.cause: Belt tension naturally loosened with use over time
probable_causes.test: Pluck belt like guitar string — should resonate clearly, not be dead

probable_causes.cause: Belt tensioner adjustment screw backed out
probable_causes.test: Check tensioner adjustment screw; measure belt deflection

probable_causes.cause: Belt teeth worn allowing slip on pulley
probable_causes.test: Inspect belt teeth for wear, cracking, or rounding

steps.instruction: Tighten X belt: turn tensioner screw clockwise or pull and retighten belt end
steps.verification: Belt has firm tension — approximately 120Hz when plucked (or per printer spec)

steps.instruction: Tighten Y belt in same manner
steps.verification: Y belt at same tension as X belt

steps.instruction: Print XY calibration square and measure for squareness
steps.verification: Diagonals within 0.2mm of each other

problems.name: Loose Screws / Fasteners
problems.description: Fasteners have loosened causing mechanical play or vibration during printing
visual_symptoms: Increased ringing or ghosting artifact on prints
Audible rattling or knocking during print moves
Layer shifting or wobble that isn't solved by belt tension

probable_causes.cause: Vibration loosening screws over time (no threadlock applied)
probable_causes.test: Systematically check all axis eccentric nuts, carriage screws, hotend mount screws

probable_causes.cause: Eccentric nuts (wheels) not properly tightened
probable_causes.test: Try to wobble X/Y gantry by hand — any play?

probable_causes.cause: Hotend or extruder mounting screws loose
probable_causes.test: Try to wiggle hotend assembly by hand — any movement?

steps.instruction: Systematically tighten all visible screws on printer frame, gantry, and carriage
steps.verification: No screws turn further; no visible play in components

steps.instruction: Adjust eccentric nuts for V-slot wheels — wheels should roll without play
steps.verification: Gantry moves smoothly without wobble

steps.instruction: Apply blue threadlock (Loctite 243) to vibration-prone fasteners
steps.verification: Fasteners won't loosen from vibration

problems.name: Z Wobble
problems.description: Z axis oscillates or wobbles during movement causing vertical banding on prints
visual_symptoms: Repeating helical or sinusoidal pattern of bands on print walls
Z banding that follows a helical path matching lead screw pitch
One side of print has more pronounced banding than other

probable_causes.cause: Z lead screw not aligned with motor shaft (bent coupling)
probable_causes.test: Watch coupling during Z movement — does it flex or wobble?

probable_causes.cause: Lead screw constrained too rigidly at top — needs freedom to float
probable_causes.test: Is lead screw fixed at top bearing? Remove or loosen top constraint

probable_causes.cause: Z axis frame not square — lead screw not vertical
probable_causes.test: Check frame with a square — is Z axis perpendicular to bed?

probable_causes.cause: Lead screw physically bent
probable_causes.test: Remove lead screw and roll on flat surface to check straightness

steps.instruction: Loosen Z motor coupling and realign lead screw with motor shaft; retighten
steps.verification: Coupling no longer flexes during Z movement

steps.instruction: If top bearing constraint: loosen or remove to let lead screw self-align
steps.verification: Lead screw runs centered without side force

steps.instruction: Install spider/flexible coupling to absorb minor misalignment
steps.verification: Z banding reduced on test print

problems.name: Abnormal Noise During Printing
problems.description: Unusual sounds such as grinding, squeaking, or knocking during printer operation
visual_symptoms: Grinding noise from X/Y axis during movement
Squeaking from bearings or linear rails
Knocking or clicking sounds at specific positions or during Z moves

probable_causes.cause: Dry linear bearings or rails needing lubrication
probable_causes.test: Apply PTFE/lithium grease to rails and bearings; does noise stop?

probable_causes.cause: V-slot wheels too tight causing friction
probable_causes.test: Loosen eccentric nuts slightly — do wheels roll more freely?

probable_causes.cause: Belt rubbing on frame or misaligned
probable_causes.test: Visually inspect belt path — does belt contact frame anywhere?

probable_causes.cause: Loose or failing motor bearing
probable_causes.test: Isolate motor by disconnecting belt — does grinding sound persist when motor moves?

steps.instruction: Apply lubricant to linear rods/rails and bearings (PTFE spray or lithium grease)
steps.verification: Axes move more quietly and smoothly

steps.instruction: Inspect belt path for rubbing contact points with frame
steps.verification: Belt runs freely without contact

steps.instruction: Check wheel tension on V-slot profiles — adjust eccentric nuts
steps.verification: Wheels roll without grinding

problems.name: Extruder Clicking / Skipping
problems.description: Extruder motor makes rhythmic clicking sounds indicating it is skipping steps
visual_symptoms: Audible click or knock from extruder area at regular intervals
Under-extrusion visible on print corresponding to click timing
Filament dust or shavings accumulating near extruder gear

probable_causes.cause: Partial or full nozzle clog creating back-pressure
probable_causes.test: Remove Bowden from hotend and try extruding — does clicking stop?

probable_causes.cause: Print temperature too low increasing viscosity
probable_causes.test: Increase temperature 5-10°C and observe if clicking reduces

probable_causes.cause: Print speed too fast for extruder to keep up
probable_causes.test: Reduce speed by 30% and check if clicking stops

probable_causes.cause: Extruder motor current too low
probable_causes.test: Check TMC driver current for extruder — compare to manufacturer spec

steps.instruction: Increase nozzle temperature by 5-10°C
steps.verification: Clicking reduces or stops

steps.instruction: Reduce print speed by 20-25%
steps.verification: Extruder feeds smoothly without clicking

steps.instruction: Check for nozzle clog: attempt cold pull to clear partial blockage
steps.verification: Filament flows freely without back-pressure

problems.name: Print Not Sticking to Bed Surface
problems.description: Print loses adhesion to bed surface despite correct Z offset, often mid-print
visual_symptoms: Print slides across bed surface during fast moves
Print detaches cleanly from bed surface without taking bed material with it
No visible adhesion failure in first layer — detachment happens later

probable_causes.cause: Bed surface contaminated with grease or release agent
probable_causes.test: Clean with IPA 90%+ — does water bead on surface? (indicates contamination)

probable_causes.cause: Wrong bed surface for material (PEI good for PLA/PETG, not flexible sheets for ABS)
probable_causes.test: Check material/surface compatibility chart for your bed type

probable_causes.cause: Bed surface worn or scratched — low adhesion spots
probable_causes.test: Inspect surface for shiny smooth spots that indicate wear

probable_causes.cause: Print moving from inertia at high speed
probable_causes.test: Reduce print speed and acceleration for tall prints

steps.instruction: Thoroughly clean bed with IPA 90%+ and lint-free cloth
steps.verification: Bed surface uniformly matte; water doesn't bead

steps.instruction: Apply adhesive for problematic materials: glue stick, Magigoo, or hairspray
steps.verification: Thin adhesive layer covers print area

steps.instruction: Verify bed temperature is correct for material and surface type
steps.verification: Correct temperature reached before printing

problems.name: Filament Tangled on Spool
problems.description: Filament loops over itself on spool, binding and stopping extrusion mid-print
visual_symptoms: Print stops suddenly without nozzle clog or other apparent issue
Filament appears locked or stuck when trying to feed by hand
Visible crossed-over or looped filament strands on the spool

probable_causes.cause: Filament end lost into spool during handling
probable_causes.test: Inspect spool — is filament end tucked under wound layers?

probable_causes.cause: Improperly wound spool from manufacturer
probable_causes.test: Inspect spool windings for crossed layers

probable_causes.cause: Filament end not secured when removing spool from printer
probable_causes.test: Did filament end spring free and loop back when last removed?

steps.instruction: Pause print immediately
steps.verification: Print paused

steps.instruction: Carefully unspool filament by hand until tangle is resolved
steps.verification: Filament feeds freely from spool without resistance

steps.instruction: Feed filament back through path and resume print
steps.verification: Extrusion resumes normally

steps.instruction: After print: always secure filament end in spool notch or clip
steps.verification: Filament end secured for next use

problems.name: Print Detaching Mid-Print
problems.description: Print separates from bed or support structure during printing, causing print failure
visual_symptoms: Print suddenly moves or spins freely when nozzle touches it
Spaghetti visible — nozzle extruding into air where print should be
Print knocked over with nozzle continuing to extrude on top of failed mass

probable_causes.cause: Insufficient bed adhesion for print height/weight
probable_causes.test: Was brim used? How large is print footprint relative to its height?

probable_causes.cause: Nozzle collision with print (layer shifted or blob)
probable_causes.test: Inspect failed print for collision damage on upper layers

probable_causes.cause: Warping forces overcoming bed adhesion
probable_causes.test: Were corners lifting before final detachment?

probable_causes.cause: Vibration or external impact during print
probable_causes.test: Check printer environment for vibration sources or accidental contact

steps.instruction: Stop print and clear failed print from bed
steps.verification: Bed clean and ready

steps.instruction: Add brim (8-10mm wide) in slicer for next attempt
steps.verification: Brim visible in slicer preview

steps.instruction: Clean bed with IPA and apply adhesive if needed
steps.verification: Bed surface clean and adhesive applied

steps.instruction: Enable Z-hop and reduce print speed to avoid nozzle collisions
steps.verification: Z-hop enabled in slicer; reduced speed set
